_comment: JFrog AI Catalog Demo — Project and Connection Definitions

demo_project.description: Demo project for the AI Catalog governance walkthrough. Scopes AI model access and MCP server usage to the ML Code Review team.

demo_connections.name: ml-openai-connection
demo_connections.description: OpenAI API connection for the ml-code-review project
demo_connections._note: Each provider-project pair requires a unique connection. This connection allows models from OpenAI to be allowed into the ml-code-review project and enables developers to call the AI Gateway with a JFrog project token.

demo_connections.name: ml-huggingface-connection
demo_connections.description: HuggingFace API connection for the ml-code-review project
demo_connections._note: Required to allow HuggingFace models (e.g., facebook/bart-large-cnn) into the project Registry.

demo_connections.name: ml-anthropic-connection
demo_connections.description: Anthropic API connection — added during Shadow AI governance demo (Act 5)
demo_connections._note: This connection is created during the demo when Shadow AI detects unmanaged Anthropic calls and the admin allows them into the project.

demo_allowed_models.description: Primary code summarization model — approved and allowed to ml-code-review
demo_allowed_models._note: This model is allowed during Act 2. Connection ml-huggingface-connection provides the credential binding.

demo_allowed_models.description: Alternative code model — second approved option shown in Act 2
Blocked in Discovery — malicious pickle payload. Never allowed to any project.
demo_allowed_models._note: This model is shown in the blocked state in Act 2. Pre-seeded scan result.

tool_policy._note: Read-only GitHub tools allowed. All write/delete operations blocked.
JFrog platform read tools only. No creation or deletion via MCP in this project.

demo_repositories.description: Proxy and cache for Hugging Face Hub models — governed access point
Local store for approved and internally promoted AI models
Unified governed access point for all AI models. Developers pull from here.
